# Lesson Title

## What You Will Learn

- Outcome 1.
- Outcome 2.
- Outcome 3.

## Prerequisites

- Required concept 1.
- Required tool 2.

## Mental Model

Explain the topic in simple language first. Use an analogy only if it remains technically accurate.

## Deep Mechanics

Explain what happens under the hood:

- Core mechanism.
- Important data structures, protocols, algorithms, or runtime behavior.
- Constraints that shape real-world usage.

<ExampleBox title="Minimal working example" lang="bash">

```bash
echo "hello expert notes"
```

</ExampleBox>

## Production Notes

| Concern | What to Check |
| --- | --- |
| Security | Permissions, secrets, isolation, dependency risk. |
| Reliability | Retries, timeouts, backups, rollback path. |
| Performance | Bottlenecks, memory, CPU, I/O, latency. |
| Observability | Logs, metrics, traces, alerts. |
| Cost | Resource usage and operational complexity. |

## Failure Modes

| Symptom | Likely Cause | Fix |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Example failure | Example root cause | Example remediation |

## Decision Guide

- Use this when...
- Avoid this when...
- Choose an alternative when...

## Exercises

<ExerciseBox title="Basic reproduction" difficulty="Easy">
Build the smallest working example and explain each moving part.
</ExerciseBox>

<ExerciseBox title="Debug a failure" difficulty="Medium">
Break the example intentionally, capture the error, and document the fix.
</ExerciseBox>

<ExerciseBox title="Production design" difficulty="Hard">
Design a production-ready version with security, observability, and rollback notes.
</ExerciseBox>

## Self-Test

<Quiz storageKey="quiz-template-expert-lesson" :cards="[
  { q: 'What is the core mental model?', a: 'Replace with the key idea.' },
  { q: 'What is the most common production failure?', a: 'Replace with a realistic failure.' },
  { q: 'When should you avoid this approach?', a: 'Replace with a tradeoff.' }
]" />
